Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Materials and methods states the BAPT full coding sequence was from T. cuspidata with accession KC988329.1.

Source_Species.reason: The paper gives the source as Taxus cuspidata, but does not explicitly use the common name 'Japanese yew'.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Bacteria strains and plasmids section states the BAPT gene was from Taxus cuspidata.

Applied_Species.reason: The experimental species is Taxus baccata, but the common name 'English yew' is not stated in the paper.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study throughout concerns transformation and culture of Taxus baccata.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: BAPT overexpression is evaluated for enhancing paclitaxel production, and PTX is quantified in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Paclitaxel is repeatedly discussed as a taxane, and taxanes are diterpenoids; this class is directly inferable from the paper's pathway context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary states paclitaxel is one of the most effective anticancer drugs ever developed.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results and supplementary figure descriptions show PTX/taxanes were measured both inside cells and in culture medium in cell suspensions.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper explicitly discusses BAPT as part of the taxane/paclitaxel biosynthetic pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: BAPT is described as a flux-limiting gene in the late pathway, and PAM correlation plus side-chain-bearing taxanes support its role in late side-chain assembly/acyltransfer.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The full enzyme name baccatin-aminophenylpropanoyl-13-O-transferase is given in the Summary and figure legends.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper does not mention BAHD acyltransferase family/domain for BAPT.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper links BAPT to side-chain formation and late paclitaxel biosynthesis, but does not explicitly state the precise substrates 'baccatin III and side-chain donor derived from β-phenylalanine' in Results.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper does not explicitly state BAPT's immediate product as '3'-N-debenzoyltaxol/side-chain-bearing taxanes', though this is broadly consistent with pathway knowledge.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The exact reaction description is not explicitly presented in the paper, only BAPT's role as a late bottleneck enzyme in side-chain-bearing taxane formation is implied.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Results/Discussion place BAPT among downstream flux-limiting genes and discuss it as a late step in taxane biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: BAPT is presented as a flux-limiting gene whose overexpression increases paclitaxel, supporting that it forms an immediate late precursor required for PTX production.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Summary explicitly calls BAPT one of the bottleneck/flux-limiting genes in the taxane pathway.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results report elicited BAPT line reached 134.01 mg/L PTX at day 16 and total taxanes peaked at 181.56 mg/L, lower and later than DBTNBT.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper reports stable transformation with R. rhizogenes, genomic PCR confirmation, RT-qPCR expression analysis, and HPLC taxane profiling in root-derived cell suspensions under control/elicited conditions.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points are reflected in Results: BAPT transgenic line generated under 35S, BAPT peak expression 59.72-fold at 48 h, PTX reached 134.01 mg/L at day 16, DBTNBT performed better/earlier, and PAM was specifically enhanced in BAPT line.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results state BAPT was very strongly expressed in the BAPT line, with maxima at 12 h and 48 h under elicitation, strongest at 48 h.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Materials and methods and Results state both transgenes were under one copy of the constitutive CaMV35S promoter.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Cell suspensions were elicited with 1 µM coronatine plus 50 mM randomly methylated-β-cyclodextrins in a two-stage culture system.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper discusses correlated PAM gene expression, but does not identify PAM as a protein-protein interactor of BAPT.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Figure 3/Results show lower final FW of control BAPT than WT, and BAPT had lower/later PTX peak than DBTNBT.

Epigenetic_Regulation.reason: Discussion states low endogenous BAPT/DBTNBT expression has epigenetic origin due to methylation in the Y-patch promoter region, and transformed copies may lack this methylation.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states the BAPT transgene was from T. cuspidata and effects were measured in T. baccata; it also reports isolated expression peaks for many pathway genes in the BAPT line.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion supports BAPT as useful for improving paclitaxel production with elicitation, though less effective than DBTNBT as a single-gene strategy.

corrections.reason: The paper does not mention BAHD acyltransferase family/domain for BAPT.
The paper discusses correlated PAM gene expression, but does not identify PAM as a protein-protein interactor of BAPT.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Materials and methods states the DBTNBT full coding sequence from T. media had accession AY563629.1.

Source_Species.reason: The paper specifies T. media/Taxus media but not the common name; moreover the JSON common name may refer to hybrid yew, not explicitly given.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper gives 'T. media' in one place, but not explicitly the typographically exact 'Taxus × media'.

Applied_Species.reason: The study species is Taxus baccata, but the common name 'English yew' is not stated.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The transformed and analysed cultures are Taxus baccata.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: DBTNBT overexpression was directly linked to paclitaxel accumulation, and PTX was quantified in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Paclitaxel is treated as a taxane and taxane pathway product; diterpenoid taxane is directly inferable from the paper context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary describes paclitaxel as an anticancer drug.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results quantify taxanes/PTX in both cells and culture medium for cell suspensions.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: DBTNBT is explicitly described as a bottleneck gene in the taxane/paclitaxel biosynthetic pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: DBTNBT is the N-benzoyltransferase acting at the terminal benzoylation step leading to paclitaxel, as reflected by enzyme name and pathway discussion.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The full name 3'-N-debenzoyltaxol N-benzoyltransferase is explicitly given.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper does not mention BAHD acyltransferase family/domain for DBTNBT.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper's enzyme name implies 3'-N-debenzoyltaxol, but it does not explicitly state the substrate pair '3'-N-debenzoyltaxol and benzoyl donor' in Results or methods.

Primary_Product.reason: From the enzyme name and pathway context, DBTNBT directly forms paclitaxel; this is directly inferable and central to the paper.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The enzyme name and pathway framing support that DBTNBT catalyzes final N-benzoylation of 3'-N-debenzoyltaxol to paclitaxel.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: DBTNBT is presented as a terminal/very late bottleneck step in paclitaxel biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: DBTNBT is directly connected to PTX formation and overexpression increased PTX strongly.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Summary explicitly identifies DBTNBT as a bottleneck/flux-limiting gene.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results report elicited DBTNBT line accumulated 308.15 mg/L PTX at day 8 and 367 mg/L total taxanes; Summary reports about 310 mg/L and >4-fold WT.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The study used stable R. rhizogenes transformation, genomic PCR for 35S-DBTNBT and marker genes, RT-qPCR, and HPLC metabolite profiling in roots and root-derived cell suspensions.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All points match Results: 35S-driven DBTNBT line generated, transcript peak 61.93-fold at 72 h, strongest PTX/total taxane increase with early day-8 maximum, and gradual GGPPS/TXS/DBAT induction.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results show constitutively elevated DBTNBT expression in control DBTNBT line with further elicitation-induced maxima at 48 and 72 h.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The inserted DBTNBT copy was under the constitutive CaMV35S promoter.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Cell culture elicitation was with 1 µM coronatine plus 50 mM randomly methylated-β-cyclodextrins in the two-stage system.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: GGPPS, TXS, and DBAT were transcriptionally responsive genes, not demonstrated protein interactors of DBTNBT.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results state elicitation most strongly reduced biomass in DBTNBT line, while viability stayed above 70% in all lines.

Epigenetic_Regulation.reason: Discussion attributes low endogenous DBTNBT/BAPT expression to promoter methylation in the Y-patch region and suggests transformed copies may escape this.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states DBTNBT transgene was from T. media and effects measured in T. baccata; Discussion infers DBTNBT overexpression may reduce feedback repression of other genes.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion concludes DBTNBT overcame the bottleneck more effectively and gave the highest paclitaxel production, supporting high engineering value.

corrections.reason: The paper does not mention BAHD acyltransferase family/domain for DBTNBT.
GGPPS, TXS, and DBAT were transcriptionally responsive genes, not demonstrated protein interactors of DBTNBT.
